name: Great House Citadel Circle
summary: The public institutional ring through which Psanan’s Great Houses negotiate precedence, obligation, and production.
day_to_day: Hosts house councils, disputes over precedence, and coordination of major civic or martial labor.
who_works_there: House clerks, heralds, oath keepers, matriarchal envoys, security retainers.
who_uses_it: Great Houses, their sworn workers, and parties entangled in house obligations.
authority: Can shape precedence, sponsorship, and public obligation, though house rivalry persists.
public_manifestation: Citadel councils, banners, escorted processions, posted house rulings.
conflict_or_bottleneck: Institutional unity weakens whenever house competition turns shortages into leverage.
